title: Making Requests and Stating Actions
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences using the Te-form to describe ongoing actions (~te imasu) and make polite requests (~te kudasai).
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences by translating the English prompts. You will need to conjugate verbs into the Te-form and attach either 'imasu' (for ongoing actions) or 'kudasai' (for requests).

contentItems.text: Mentally identify the verb's dictionary form first. Example: 'To wait' is Machimasu. It is Group 1. 'chi' becomes 'tte'. -> Matte.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) I am writing a letter now. 2) Please speak Japanese. 3) I am reading a book. 4) Please wait a little.
promptGoal: To construct complex verbs combining Te-form + imasu and Te-form + kudasai.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: いま てがみを かいて います。にほんごを はなして ください。ほんを よんで います。ちょっと まって ください。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Ima tegami wo kaite imasu. Nihongo wo hanashite kudasai. Hon wo yonde imasu. Chotto matte kudasai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I am writing a letter now. Please speak Japanese. I am reading a book. Please wait a little.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect conjugation of four different Group 1 verbs (ki->ite, shi->shite, mi->nde, chi->tte).

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'kaite imasu' for writing?
Did the learner write 'hanashite kudasai' for speaking?
Did the learner write 'yonde imasu' for reading?
Did the learner write 'matte kudasai' for waiting?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: いま てがみを かいて います。にほんごを はなして ください。ほんを よんで います。ちょっと まって ください。
acceptableAlternates: わたしは いま てがみを かいて います。にほんごを はなして ください。わたしは ほんを よんで います。ちょっと まって ください。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Flawless execution of four different Group 1 Te-form rules.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You have mastered the most difficult conjugation rules in Japanese.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the Group 1 song! Kakimasu = ki = ite = Kaite. Yomimasu = mi = nde = Yonde.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't mix up 'kudasai' (please) with 'imasu' (is doing).

hintText: Break it down. 'Wait' = Machimasu. It ends in 'chi'. The rule is 'i, chi, ri -> tte'. So it becomes 'Matte'.
